_note: ORIGINAL content. javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript informed topic coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Operators
summary: Combine values with arithmetic, comparison, and logic.
refresher: Arithmetic operators calculate values, comparisons produce booleans, and logical operators join conditions. Prefer `===` so type and value must match.

blocks.body: Arithmetic operators calculate values, comparisons produce booleans, and logical operators join conditions. Prefer `===` so type and value must match. Combine values with arithmetic, comparison, and logic.

Operators work in a particular order. Multiplication and division happen before addition and subtraction, then comparisons produce booleans that logical operators can combine. JavaScript also chooses behavior from operand types, so plus adds two numbers but joins text when either side is a string.

blocks.caption: Multiplication runs before addition, then strict equality tests the completed result.
Explicit conversion makes the intended operation visible.

blocks.body: Trace an expression in small pieces. For `2 + 4 * 3 === 14`, calculate `4 * 3`, add two, and compare fourteen with fourteen. Each intermediate result has both a value and a type.

blocks.title: Watch this detail
blocks.body: Loose equality may convert an operand first, so `5 == "5"` is true while `5 === "5"` is false.

blocks.why: Multiplication produces six before that value is subtracted from ten.
The variable holds a string, so it is strictly unequal to the number seven.
